title: ABC谋杀案
summary:
Hercule Poirot and Hastings interview Mrs. Fowler, a neighbor of the deceased Alice Ascher, paying her five pounds for her information. Mrs. Fowler describes Mrs. Ascher as a private person who struggled with the behavior and financial demands of her alcoholic husband, Franz Ascher, whom she characterized as a "beast" who had threatened to ruin her. When questioned about the "ABC" letters or train timetables, Mrs. Fowler denies seeing any. She also suggests that Franz likely hid himself well to avoid detection for the murder, noting that the house lacks a back door.

Inspector Glen joins the pair and expresses frustration with the investigation. He reports that while witnesses saw various groups of strangers near the shop—including tall, shifty men and short, bearded men—no one witnessed a group of masked men with revolvers. Additionally, no one has reported seeing Mr. Ascher.

The investigation continues with an interview of Mr. James Patrick, a thin, precise bank clerk who claims to be the last person to see Mrs. Ascher alive. Patrick states he arrived at her shop at 5:30 PM, a time he verified by checking his watch against the church bell. He claims to know nothing of Mrs. Ascher’s personal life or her husband's alcoholism. As they leave, Poirot notices an "ABC" British Rail timetable on a shelf in Patrick's home and takes it under the pretense of checking train schedules. Finally, they attempt to speak with Albert Riddle, a hostile railway worker, but he refuses to cooperate, stating he has already provided all the information he has to the police.
